speaker: CASSIO
text: Masters, play here (I will content your pains)

speaker: CASSIO
text: Something that’s brief; and bid “Good morrow, general.”

speaker: CLOWN
text: Why masters, have your instruments been in

speaker: CLOWN
text: Naples, that they speak i’ th’ nose thus?

speaker: MUSICIAN
text: How, sir, how?

speaker: CLOWN
text: Are these, I pray you, wind instruments?

speaker: MUSICIAN
text: Ay, marry, are they, sir.

speaker: CLOWN
text: O, thereby hangs a tail.

speaker: MUSICIAN
text: Whereby hangs a tale, sir?

speaker: CLOWN
text: Marry, sir, by many a wind instrument that I

speaker: CLOWN
text: know. But, masters, here’s money for you; and the

speaker: CLOWN
text: General so likes your music that he desires you, for

speaker: CLOWN
text: love’s sake, to make no more noise with it.

speaker: MUSICIAN
text: Well, sir, we will not.

speaker: CLOWN
text: If you have any music that may not be heard, to

speaker: CLOWN
text: ’t again. But, as they say, to hear music the General

speaker: CLOWN
text: does not greatly care.

speaker: MUSICIAN
text: We have none such, sir.

speaker: CLOWN
text: Then put up your pipes in your bag, for I’ll

speaker: CLOWN
text: away. Go, vanish into air, away!

speaker: CASSIO
text: Dost thou hear, mine honest friend?

speaker: CLOWN
text: No, I hear not your honest friend. I hear you.

speaker: CASSIO
text: Prithee, keep up thy quillets.

speaker: CASSIO
text: There’s a poor piece of gold for thee. If the gentlewoman

speaker: CASSIO
text: that attends the General’s wife be stirring,

speaker: CASSIO
text: tell her there’s one Cassio entreats her a little favor

speaker: CASSIO
text: of speech. Wilt thou do this?

speaker: CLOWN
text: She is stirring, sir. If she will stir hither, I shall

speaker: CLOWN
text: seem to notify unto her.

speaker: CASSIO
text: Do, good my friend.

speaker: CASSIO
text: In happy time, Iago.

speaker: IAGO
text: You have not been abed, then?

speaker: CASSIO
text: Why, no. The day had broke

speaker: CASSIO
text: Before we parted. I have made bold, Iago,

speaker: CASSIO
text: To send in to your wife. My suit to her

speaker: CASSIO
text: Is that she will to virtuous Desdemona

speaker: CASSIO
text: Procure me some access.

speaker: IAGO
text: I’ll send her to you presently,

speaker: IAGO
text: And I’ll devise a mean to draw the Moor

speaker: IAGO
text: Out of the way, that your converse and business

speaker: IAGO
text: May be more free.

speaker: CASSIO
text:
I humbly thank you for ’t.
                 
                Iago
                 
                exits
                .
               I never knew

speaker: CASSIO
text: A Florentine more kind and honest.

speaker: EMILIA
text: Good morrow, good lieutenant. I am sorry

speaker: EMILIA
text: For your displeasure, but all will sure be well.

speaker: EMILIA
text: The General and his wife are talking of it,

speaker: EMILIA
text: And she speaks for you stoutly. The Moor replies

speaker: EMILIA
text: That he you hurt is of great fame in Cyprus

speaker: EMILIA
text: And great affinity, and that in wholesome wisdom

speaker: EMILIA
text: He might not but refuse you. But he protests he loves you

speaker: EMILIA
text: And needs no other suitor but his likings

speaker: EMILIA
text: To take the safest occasion by the front

speaker: EMILIA
text: To bring you in again.

speaker: CASSIO
text: Yet I beseech you,

speaker: CASSIO
text: If you think fit, or that it may be done,

speaker: CASSIO
text: Give me advantage of some brief discourse

speaker: CASSIO
text: With Desdemon alone.

speaker: EMILIA
text: Pray you come in.

speaker: EMILIA
text: I will bestow you where you shall have time

speaker: EMILIA
text: To speak your bosom freely.

speaker: CASSIO
text: I am much bound to you.
